claims.claim: Egg consumption significantly increases the LDL-c/HDL-c ratio and LDL-c levels, especially with a longer intervention duration.
Egg consumption did not effectively increase the HDL-c level.
The study focused on healthy subjects to avoid bias from conceptual heterogeneity, and results may be more appropriate for a healthy population.
The intervention duration of the included studies were all within a year, so results for longer intervention times cannot be concluded.
